title: Squalene Quantification using Nile Red Staining (M4455 Version)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9rcf4","text":"This protocol is under development and for teaching purposes only!","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":66}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9pg21","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9q1ot","text":"Nile Red is a fluorescent dye that stains selectively hydrophobic substances. We assume that squalene accumulates in the cell membrane or in lipid vesicles where it can be stained by the dye. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7to83","text":"This protocol is under development and for teaching purposes only!","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":66}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"2latl","text":"This protocol is under development and for teaching purposes only!","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":66}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Sample 1-2 ml Synechocystis culture, measure its OD at 750 nm and adjust it to 2 ml of OD (750 nm) = 0.2 in BG-11 media. Split your adjusted culture in 2x 1 ml . One portion will be stained with Nile Red, the other will serve as a negative control.
Stain one portion of the previously adjusted culture with c (final) = 10 µg/ml Nile Red . Wear gloves and a lab coat when working with DMSO as it stains skin and cloth.
Incubate your culures for 30 min in the dark. 00:30:00, Incubation
Centrifuge all samples for 5 min at 5000 xg . 00:05:00, Centrifugation
Carefully discard the supernatant by pipetting. Avoid resuspending the pellet or losing cells! Try removing as much supernatant as possible.
Thoroughly resuspend all pellets in 1 ml BG-11 media .
Fill each 200 µl in 96 well plate's wells. Your sample volume should allow four wells per sample.
Measure each well's OD(750 nm) in the plate reader.
Measure Nile Red fluorescence. Use following wavelenghts: Excitation: 510 nm/Emission : 660 nm

Analyze your data: Normalize each well's fluorescence to the respective OD and subtract fluorescence without Nile Red from fluorescence with Nile Red. Compare your results to wild type Nile Red stained fluorescence.
creator: Sebastian ST Triesch
